note: All figures from the Durham County FY2022-2031 CIP and City of Durham FY2025-26 Adopted Budget. Durham County issues bonds for DPS, Durham Tech, and Museum of Life and Science but does not manage their construction.

projects.title: Water & Sewer Infrastructure
projects.description: Upgrades to water treatment plants, sewer lines, pump stations, and aging pipe replacements to support population growth and maintain system reliability.
projects.locationNote: Program-level: projects throughout City of Durham

projects.title: GoDurham Transit Expansion
projects.description: New buses, transit facility improvements, and expanded service routes for the GoDurham city bus system.
projects.locationNote: Program-level: projects throughout City of Durham

projects.title: Stormwater Improvements
projects.description: Stormwater drainage upgrades, stream restoration, and flood-prevention infrastructure to protect Durham neighborhoods.
projects.locationNote: Program-level: projects throughout City of Durham

projects.title: Street Repaving Program
projects.description: Resurfacing and reconstruction of city streets, prioritizing the most deteriorated roads to reduce long-term maintenance costs.
projects.locationNote: Program-level: projects throughout City of Durham

projects.title: Parks – Lead Soil Remediation
projects.description: Removal of lead-contaminated soil at city parks identified in a 2024 survey. Priority project to protect children's health at public playgrounds.
projects.locationNote: Program-level: projects throughout City of Durham

projects.title: Sidewalk Repairs & Access
projects.description: Repairs and new sidewalk construction to improve pedestrian safety and ADA accessibility throughout Durham.
projects.locationNote: Program-level: projects throughout City of Durham

projects.title: Other City Capital Projects
projects.description: Facilities improvements, fire station renovations, technology infrastructure, and other capital investments across city departments.
projects.sourceLabel: City of Durham Finance: FY2025-26 Budget
projects.locationNote: Program-level: projects throughout City of Durham

projects.title: 2022 Bond – Durham Public Schools
projects.description: Durham County voters approved a $550.2M bond in November 2022. Of that, $423.5M goes to DPS for new school construction and major renovations: including a new Murray-Massenburg Elementary, a new Durham School of the Arts campus, and renovations at Club Boulevard, Glenn, and other schools. Durham County issued the bond; DPS manages construction.
projects.locationNote: Program-level: projects at schools throughout Durham County

projects.title: 2022 Bond – Durham Technical Community College
projects.description: Part of the November 2022 $550.2M Durham County GO Bond. $112.7M supports two new classroom buildings, repairs and renovations, and land acquisition at Durham Tech. Durham County issued the bond; Durham Tech manages construction.
projects.locationNote: Durham Technical Community College, 1637 E Lawson St

projects.title: 2022 Bond – NC Museum of Life and Science
projects.description: Part of the November 2022 $550.2M Durham County GO Bond. $13.9M supports repairs, renovations, and expansion at the NC Museum of Life and Science. Durham County issued the bond; the Museum manages construction.
projects.locationNote: NC Museum of Life and Science, 433 W Murray Ave

projects.title: Public Safety and Service Complex
projects.description: A new Public Safety and Service Complex built over most of the CIP's 10-year span using Limited Obligation Bond (LOBS) financing. Part of Durham County's investment in public safety infrastructure.
projects.locationNote: Approximate: final site to be determined

projects.title: Downtown Parking Decks with Affordable Housing
projects.description: Two new downtown parking decks incorporating affordable housing units, financed through Limited Obligation Bonds. Supports downtown growth while expanding affordable housing in the urban core.

projects.title: Snow Hill Road Pump Station
projects.description: A new pump station on Snow Hill Road to support wastewater infrastructure in northeast Durham, financed through Limited Obligation Bonds.

projects.title: Youth Home
projects.description: A new Durham County Youth Home to replace the existing facility, providing safe, modern housing and services for youth in the county's care. Financed through Limited Obligation Bonds.
